summary: Well-structured product ideal describing a graph-based visualization and round-trip editing system for Agentish documents. Clean separation into Source, Semantics, Projection, and Editing namespaces with explicit Truth invariants that each carry a .means() clause. Causal chains (open→normalize→project, edit→validate→mutate→apply, conflict→surface→protect) are complete and recoverable without companion files. Moderate obsolescence risk because no concrete implementation or active reference evidence is visible from provided context.
strengths: Causal flow through four when/then blocks covers the complete read-edit-conflict lifecycle without gaps
Truth declarations with .means() clauses eliminate interpretive slack on core invariants like source authority and round-trip editing
Namespace grouping (Source, Semantics, Identity, Projection, Editing, Truth) gives immediate structural legibility
Stays cleanly at product-ideal abstraction—no implementation leakage into rendering, parsing, or storage
Fully self-describing: a reader with only the language root can reconstruct the entire concept
weaknesses: SemanticModel described only as 'normalized meaning'—does not preload what normalization entails or why it matters for projection fidelity
No failure-mode or degradation when/then blocks—the language root values ActivationPreload of failure modes but this file omits them (e.g., what happens when round-trip mutation is lossy?)
Identity.stable is declared but its relationship to SemanticModel is only structural (contains) without causal explanation of how identity is derived or preserved across edits
No live-use evidence visible: no references from implementation files, no companion state file, no changelog signals
